historical_error: dial tcp: lookup supabase-mail on 127.0.0.11:53: server misbehaving
historical_root_cause: Supabase Auth originally used placeholder SMTP config (supabase-mail:2500 with fake sender values), but no supabase-mail service existed on the Docker network, so SEND_OTP failed before a real SMTP session could start.
root_cause: The real failure was not bad credentials alone; it was a nonexistent SMTP target. Once GoTrue stopped using supabase-mail:2500 and switched to a reachable local SMTP bridge at 172.18.0.1:2500, OTP delivery and VERIFY_OTP succeeded.
recommended_fix: Keep the old environment on the repaired SMTP path for now, complete Waffo payment/webhook E2E there, and plan California migration later as a controlled rebuild instead of using SMTP as the trigger for immediate migration.
notes: Current magic link emails are generated against pay-api.915500.xyz, not weiwang.915500.xyz.
pay-api.915500.xyz is not present in GOTRUE_URI_ALLOW_LIST, but it is present in GOTRUE_MAILER_EXTERNAL_HOSTS and is the correct API host for the magic link.
Auth logs still show a non-blocking x_forwarded_host=kong warning for internal proxy traffic.
